why.1: Frontmatter has name: sanitizing-customer-exports first, then description: as the second field, matching the folder name.
why.2: Description starts with 'Use when' and ends with an immediate instruction ('Run scripts/redact_export.py...; never hand-edit or share the original'), not a passive capability blurb.
why.3: Body steps are imperative (run the script, share only the redacted file, confirm wrote..., spot-check, send only .redacted.csv, never share/modify the original), not 'this skill does X' prose.
why.4: No CSV/PII definitions and no overview restating the description; body opens with '## Redact a customer export' and the run command.
why.5: Exact column rules are tabulated (email/phone/ssn dropped; name → opaque id; notes/comments free-text masking) and output path is fixed as <stem>.redacted.csv, never in-place edit.
why.6: Only SKILL.md, scripts/redact_export.py, and one examples/sample_export.csv are provided. No evals/evals.json (or equivalent) with ≥3 cases covering normal, edge, and out-of-scope inputs.

why.2: Rewritten description starts with 'Use when validating a JSON configuration file for syntax errors or schema conformance' — trigger phrase plus concrete activation conditions; vacuous 'Use when needed' is gone.
why.3: Rewrite has no JSON-primer Introduction. Review flags it as content Claude already knows ('pure token cost with no payoff').
why.4: Rewrite body is procedural (headings + code + report instruction) with no 'Amazing', 'incredibly powerful', 'fantastic time-saver', or exclamation hype.
why.5: Passive 'How It Works' hedging is replaced by imperative sections with exact Python snippets using json.load and jsonschema.validate, plus a concrete error-reporting rule.
why.6: Review targets missing WHEN/WHAT: no real trigger, no indication of what is checked (syntax? schema?), wastes description budget instead of telling when to invoke and what it does — not merely informal/long.
